mnemonics: For Groups, Remember A4: A set with an Associative operation that has an Identity and Inverses for All elements.
keyPoints: A group is a set equipped with a binary operation that is associative, has an identity element, and every element has an inverse.
Groups are a fundamental algebraic structure used to model symmetry and many other concepts in mathematics and computer science.
The integers under addition (Z, +) form a classic example of an infinite group.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check the closure property; the result of the operation on any two elements must also be in the set.
Assuming commutativity; a group does not have to be abelian (commutative). The operation only needs to be associative.
